title: Inference, Annotations, Literals, and Widening · Transfer Build
summary: Model service categories, thresholds, identifiers, and configuration without widening away domain meaning. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a literal-safe service configuration model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
